channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - gameinformer.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for gameinformer.com

item.title: PS5 Exclusive Destruction AllStars Has Been Delisted And Is Shutting Down This Fall
item.content:encoded:
Destruction AllStars, the multiplayer vehicular arena combat game, was among the PlayStation 5’s first exclusive titles, and now it’s time to bid it farewell. The game is shutting down this Fall and has already been removed from sale.

Players are receiving emails from PlayStation notifying them that, as of today, Destruction AllStars has been delisted from the PlayStation Store along with its Destruction Points virtual currency. Additionally, the game’s multiplayer services have been disabled for good.

However, owners can still play the game’s single-player modes until November 25, when, at 11:00 a.m. ET, all server support will cease. After this date, Arcade Mode single-player challenges will remain playable, though PlayStation warns that its functionality and the overall experience may be impacted by the server shutdown. Remaining Destruction Points owned by players can be redeemed in the available single-player modes until November 25.

Destruction AllStars launched on February 2, 2021, and was developed by Lucid Games. The game lets players step into the driver’s seats of wacky vehicles to battle other drivers in over-the-top destruction derby matches. In addition to using the car as a weapon, the driver could exit vehicles to roam freely and even steal an opponent’s car. It was pretty nuts, and we liked it enough to score it an 8 out of 10 when it launched, with reviewer and former GI editor Jeff Cork writing, “Even if the novelty of vehicular mayhem isn’t enough to sustain my interest for many more long-haul sessions, I’m looking forward to taking it for a quick spin every now and again.”

Unfortunately, Destruction AllStars is far from the only multiplayer game having its lights turned off this year. For related stories, read about how Bungie is ending active development for Destiny 2 in June, then read about the total shutdowns of Spellcasters Chronicles and Highguard. 



https://gameinformer.com/2026/05/26/ps5-exclusive-destruction-allstars-has-been-delisted-and-is-shutting-down-this-fall
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon Quest 40th Anniversary Stream Will Feature New Announcements Tomorrow
item.content:encoded:
Square Enix has announced a special livestream celebrating the 40th anniversary of Dragon Quest. The presentation will air tomorrow, May 27, to celebrate Dragon Quest Day and will feature announcements related to the series.

The roughly 10-minute broadcast will be streamed on YouTube starting at 6 a.m. PT/9 a.m. ET. We don't know what the announcements will be, but I’m sure the number one game on every fan’s mind will be the long-awaited Dragon Quest XII: The Flames of Fate. The next mainline entry was first announced in 2021 (for Dragon Quest’s 35th anniversary, funny enough), but it has made nary a peep since then and has yet to receive a release window. Could tomorrow be the day that finally changes? 

Square Enix has already released two Dragon Quest titles this year in the forms of Dragon Quest VII Reimagined and the mobile-exclusive Dragon Quest Smash/Grow. It’s possible Square could announce content updates for one or both of these games. Beyond that, it’s anyone’s guess what to expect. Another HD-2D remake of an older Dragon Quest game? Dragon Quest Builders 3? A new Slime spin-off? Get your wishlists ready now, and let us know what you'd like to see in the comments. 

You can also keep up with every video game broadcast streaming this summer with our 2026 Summer Showcase Schedule. 



https://gameinformer.com/2026/05/26/dragon-quest-40th-anniversary-stream-will-feature-new-announcements-tomorrow
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
